note: Supersedes previous PIVOT decision. New evidence from C2 v9 (GO), B1_pairwise_eda (cos_enc_p_dec_c AUROC=0.730), and F1 revised theory warrants ADVANCE on revised cand_a framing.

surviving_contributions: EDA AUROC=0.681 (Cohen's d=+0.70, p=1.6e-04): strong probe-free detector
cos_enc_p_dec_c AUROC=0.730 (Cohen's d=0.552, p=2.8e-09): unexpected strongest detector - new finding
cos_enc_c_dec_p mean AUROC=0.681 (Cohen's d=0.517, p=2.7e-06): parent-child cross-direction signal
First-letter cross-domain absorption: ratio-to-null=10.0, C2 v9 GO (120 events)
Absorption phase stability: uniformly 0.919-0.966 across ALL 11 tested hyperparameter configs
F1 revised theory: mechanistic account for EDA growth under absorption (conjecture, labeled)
Mitigation pipeline: Standard vs. TopK EDA comparison validated
falsified_or_unsupported: ASI (cos^2 x freq_ratio): AUROC=0.421 below null (0.497) - H2/H3 FALSIFIED
RD threshold as direct predictor: AUROC=0.410 - anti-correlated
Sigmoidal phase transition: BIC diff=-3.22, LRT p=0.456 - H4a NOT SUPPORTED
Semantic hierarchy cross-domain on GPT-2 Small: absorption_rate near 0 - model-scale question, not falsification
required_modifications: Promote EDA and cos_enc_p_dec_c as primary empirical contributions; demote ASI to secondary
Reframe phase contribution as 'absorption phase stability' not 'phase transition'
Expand cross-domain to Gemma Scope for semantic hierarchies
Document EDA magnitude tension honestly in paper (F1 Part III)
Validate cos_enc_p_dec_c on Gemma Scope as novel finding

cand_b.summary: Cross-layer absorption profile is flat (0.919-0.966 all layers), falsifying the predicted 'middle-layer peak'. Key findings fold into cand_a as secondary analysis (EDA layer-dependence: L6 AUROC=0.681 vs. L10 AUROC=0.337). Not viable as standalone paper.

cand_c.summary: SAEBench/SynthSAEBench collision reduces standalone value. Viable only as supplement to cand_a.

cand_d.summary: Exact match with Tang et al. arXiv:2512.05534 v3.

key_discoveries_from_pilot: cos_enc_p_dec_c AUROC=0.730: parent encoder aligns with child decoder - strongest absorption predictor, not in original proposal
EDA=0.681 > freq_ratio=0.612 > ASI=0.421 - encoder-decoder dissociation outperforms decoder-decoder geometry
First-letter absorption confirmed with redesigned C2 v9 (ratio=10.0) - cross-domain measurement validated
Absorption is phase-stable at ~96% across ALL tested SAE hyperparameters (11 configs)
cos^2 (decoder-decoder) is ANTI-correlated with absorption (AUROC=0.206, Cohen's d=-0.48) - inverse of naive prediction
Semantic hierarchy absorption absent on GPT-2 Small L6; may differ on Gemma Scope
reasons: Three independent strong signals above null: EDA (0.681), cos_enc_p_dec_c (0.730), and first-letter absorption ratio=10.0
Proposition 1 (lambda > sin^2 theta) proven non-circularly - provides theoretical anchor

Falsified hypotheses (ASI, sigmoidal transition) strengthen rather than kill the paper: finding that encoder-decoder dissociation predicts absorption while decoder-decoder cosine anti-predicts is a genuine mechanistic contribution
cand_b falsified (flat cross-layer profile) - no viable standalone; its best findings fold into cand_a
Revised cand_a framing has clear path to NeurIPS/ICLR paper with theoretical + empirical contributions
next_actions: Run full Gemma Scope experiments (Gemma-2-2B, Gemma Scope 16k, layers 12 and 20): EDA, cos_enc_p_dec_c, cross-domain C2, scaling curve across widths
Reformulate ASI as freq_ratio alone or parent-centric ASI_p(c) before running Phase D at scale
Complete hysteresis experiment E2 full (infrastructure validated)
Complete mitigation comparison F2 full (Standard vs. TopK vs. Matryoshka if available)
Update proposal.md to reflect revised framing: EDA/cos_enc_p_dec_c as primary metrics
confidence_rationale: 0.76 reflects: (1) three independent signals clearly above null with strong effect sizes; (2) Proposition 1 proof survives; (3) C2 v9 GO is robust (120 events, ratio=10.0); (4) uncertainty reduced by: cos_enc_p_dec_c being a new unexpected finding needs Gemma Scope replication; semantic hierarchy null on GPT-2 Small is a known risk that requires Gemma Scope to resolve; (5) clear executable path exists for all required experiments.
